name: Banneker/Key Scholarship
requirements: Incoming freshman admitted to the UMD Honors College. Must apply by November 1 early action deadline. U.S. citizen or eligible non-citizen. Competitive academic record (typically top of class, exceptional test scores, demonstrated leadership). Semifinalists invited to interview.
description: UMD's most prestigious merit scholarship. Can cover up to full cost of tuition, mandatory fees, housing, food, and book allowance, and includes admission to the Honors College.

name: President's Scholarship
requirements: Incoming freshman (both in-state and out-of-state eligible). Must apply by November 1 early action deadline. U.S. citizen or eligible non-citizen. Selection based on academic achievement, extracurricular activities, awards, honors, and essay.
description: Four-year merit scholarship ranging from $2,000 to $20,000 per year. Awarded to academically talented incoming freshmen regardless of state of residence.

name: Dean's Scholarship
requirements: Incoming freshman. In-state (Maryland resident) applicants only. Must apply by November 1 early action deadline. Selection based on academic achievement, extracurriculars, awards, honors, and essay.
description: Merit scholarship of $1,500 for freshman year or $4,500 per year for freshman and sophomore years. Roughly 400 recipients each fall.

name: Frederick Douglass Scholarship
requirements: Transfer applicant from a Maryland community college. Strong academic record. Separate application required. U.S. citizen or permanent resident.
description: Prestigious UMD scholarship for academically talented transfer students from Maryland community colleges. Honors the life and legacy of Frederick Douglass.

name: Maryland Transfer Scholarship
requirements: Maryland resident transferring from a two-year or four-year institution. Must apply for admission by March 1 early action deadline and submit FAFSA by March 1. Must enroll full-time and maintain 3.20 cumulative GPA. Demonstrated financial need plus academic talent.
description: Partial-tuition award of $5,000 distributed over two semesters for two years. Roughly 200 recipients each fall.

name: President's Transfer Scholarship
requirements: Out-of-state transfer student. Must apply by March 1 early action deadline. U.S. citizen or permanent resident. Competitive review based on academic achievement, extracurriculars, awards, honors, and essay.
description: Annual $5,000 award for up to two years, specifically for out-of-state transfer students enrolled at UMD.

name: Terrapin Commitment
requirements: In-state (Maryland) undergraduate. Family income of $75,000 or less. Demonstrated unmet financial need. Must submit FAFSA by March 1 priority deadline.
description: UMD's promise that 100% of tuition and mandatory fees are covered for in-state students with unmet financial need from families making $75,000 or less.

name: Howard P. Rawlings Guaranteed Access Grant
requirements: Maryland resident. Current high school senior completing a college preparatory program, or GED holder under age 26. Must file FAFSA by March 1. Demonstrated high financial need. Must enroll full-time at a Maryland institution.
description: Need-based Maryland state grant providing up to $20,200 (up to 100% of financial need) for eligible low-income Maryland residents attending Maryland colleges.

name: Howard P. Rawlings Educational Assistance Grant
requirements: Maryland resident attending a Maryland public or private college. Must file FAFSA by March 1. Demonstrated financial need. Enrolled at least half-time.
description: Maryland's largest state financial aid program. Need-based grant ranging from $400 to $3,000 annually, awarded to low- and middle-income Maryland undergraduates.

name: 2+2 Transfer Scholarship
requirements: Transfer student from a Maryland community college to a four-year Maryland institution. Minimum GPA 3.0 at time of transfer. Completed at least 60 credits. Must file FAFSA.
description: State scholarship encouraging Maryland community college transfers to continue their education at four-year Maryland institutions.

name: Maryland Senatorial Scholarship
requirements: Maryland resident. Current high school senior, or full-time or part-time undergraduate/graduate student. Must file FAFSA by March 1. Awarded by your Maryland State Senator based on their criteria.
description: Legislative scholarship offered to Maryland residents attending any Maryland college, university, or private career school. All Maryland residents can apply.

name: Maryland Delegate Scholarship
requirements: Maryland resident. Plans to attend a two-year or four-year Maryland college, university, or private career school full-time or part-time. Application and criteria vary by Delegate.
description: Legislative scholarship awarded by Maryland State Delegates. May be need- or merit-based depending on the specific Delegate's criteria.

name: Teaching Fellows for Maryland Scholarship
requirements: Enrolled in a teacher preparation program leading to Maryland certification. Must pledge to teach as a Maryland public school or public pre-K teacher upon graduation. Must teach in a high-needs school OR shortage subject area (STEM, Special Education, etc.). Renewable for up to 4 years undergrad.
description: Full scholarship covering 100% of tuition, mandatory fees, and room and board for aspiring Maryland teachers. Service obligation required.

name: Workforce Shortage Student Assistance Grant
requirements: Maryland resident. Enrolled in an eligible program leading to a career in a state-designated workforce shortage area (teaching, nursing, human services, physical/occupational therapy, public service). Service obligation to work in Maryland in the designated field after graduation.
description: State grant for students entering designated workforce shortage fields. Requires a post-graduation service commitment in Maryland.

name: Charles W. Riley Firefighter and Ambulance & Rescue Squad Scholarship
requirements: Maryland resident who is an active volunteer firefighter, ambulance, or rescue squad member (or spouse/child of a deceased member). Pursuing degree or certificate in fire service technology, EMT, or related fields. Service commitment required.
description: Honors Maryland's volunteer first responders by funding their higher education in public safety fields.

name: Edward T. Conroy Memorial Scholarship
requirements: Maryland resident who is a veteran of the U.S. Armed Forces (with qualifying service), OR a son/daughter/surviving spouse of a deceased or 100% disabled veteran, OR a son/daughter of a 9/11 victim, OR a POW/MIA from the Vietnam Conflict. Attending a Maryland institution.
description: Honors Maryland veterans, service members, public safety officers, and their families by supporting their postsecondary education.

name: Maryland Tuition Waiver for Foster Care Recipients
requirements: Current or former Maryland foster care youth (exited foster care to adoption/guardianship after age 13, or aged out of care). Under age 25. Enrolled at a Maryland public institution pursuing associate, bachelor's, or vocational certificate.
description: Tuition and fee waiver at Maryland public institutions for current and former foster care youth.

name: Maryland Tuition Waiver for Homeless Youth
requirements: Maryland homeless or unaccompanied youth (as verified by a school district homeless liaison, shelter director, or similar official). Under age 25. Enrolled at a Maryland public institution pursuing associate, bachelor's, or vocational certificate.
description: Tuition and fee waiver at Maryland public institutions for students experiencing homelessness.

name: Nurse Support Program II - Hal & Jo Cohen Graduate Nursing Faculty Scholarship
requirements: Maryland resident enrolled in a graduate nursing program (MSN or DNP). Commitment to serve as nursing faculty at a Maryland nursing school after graduation. Minimum GPA 3.0.
description: Graduate scholarship for aspiring nursing faculty committed to teaching at Maryland nursing schools, addressing the nursing educator shortage.

name: Maryland Part-Time Grant
requirements: Maryland resident enrolled part-time (6-11 credits) at a Maryland postsecondary institution. Demonstrated financial need. Must file FAFSA by March 1. Not pursuing a graduate degree.
description: Need-based grant supporting part-time Maryland undergraduates, typically working adults balancing school with employment.

name: Jack F. Tolbert Memorial Student Grant
requirements: Maryland resident enrolled at an eligible Maryland private career school. Demonstrated financial need. Must file FAFSA by March 1.
description: Need-based grant for Maryland residents attending private career schools.

name: Federal Pell Grant
requirements: Undergraduate student who has not yet earned a bachelor's or professional degree. Demonstrated exceptional financial need as determined by FAFSA (Student Aid Index). U.S. citizen or eligible non-citizen. Maintain satisfactory academic progress. Lifetime limit of 12 full-time semesters.
description: The foundational federal need-based grant. Maximum award for 2025-26 is $7,395. Does not need to be repaid. Award varies based on SAI and enrollment status.

name: Federal Supplemental Educational Opportunity Grant (FSEOG)
requirements: Undergraduate student who has not yet earned a bachelor's degree. Exceptional financial need (Pell Grant recipients get priority). U.S. citizen or eligible non-citizen. Must file FAFSA. First-come, first-served once institutional funds are awarded.
description: Federal campus-based grant ranging from $100 to $4,000 per year. Awarded by colleges to students with the greatest financial need. Apply early — funds are limited.

name: Federal TEACH Grant
requirements: Enrolled in coursework necessary to begin a career in teaching. Maintain a cumulative GPA of at least 3.25. Agree to teach full-time for at least 4 academic years within 8 years of graduating, in a high-need field (math, science, special ed, bilingual ed, foreign language, reading) at a school serving low-income students. U.S. citizen or eligible non-citizen.
description: Up to $3,772 per year for aspiring teachers who commit to a service obligation. Warning: if you don't fulfill the teaching commitment, the grant converts to a loan you must repay with interest.
